system.description:
Discipline: Qinggong
Skill: Speed
Type: Counter
Qi: 6

This Technique specifically counters The Final Tranquility of the Storm, giving you an opportunity to avoid it. It can be used in natural storms as well. If your Speed roll meets or exceeds the Ritual roll of The Final Tranquility of the Storm, you dodge out of the way, and the lightning strikes the ground where you were (this may be harmless or have some effect such as starting a fire; consult the GM). If you are dodging a natural lightning strike, the GM should roll 1d10 and treat that as the original Ritual roll. After successfully using this Counter, if you find the design used for The Final Tranquility of the Storm while it is still glowing, you immediately learn the identity of whoever sent the bolt at you. Cathartic: Your body’s Speed aligns perfectly with that of the lightning, and you allow yourself to be struck, channeling the lightning through you, to your benefit. First, roll 6d10, and heal one Wound for each die that meets or exceeds your Hardiness (Total Successes heal two). For the next hour, you receive +1 Evade, +1d10 Speed, and +1d10 Athletics. Finally, the design made by The Final Tranquility of the Storm will remain for one day instead of one hour.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
